Narendra Modi.notes:
Narendra Damodardas Modi | Born 17 September 1950 | Vadnagar, Bombay State (present-day Gujarat)
Current Role | Prime Minister of India | Since 2014
Previous Role | Chief Minister of Gujarat | 2001 to 2014
Political Party | Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP)
Affiliation | Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS)
Parliamentary Constituency | Varanasi | Member of Parliament (MP)
Longest-serving PM | Outside the Indian National Congress
Education | BA in Political Science | Delhi University | 1978
Education | MA in Political Science | Gujarat University | 1983
Early Career | Full-time worker for RSS | Gujarat | 1971
Early Political Role | General Secretary of Gujarat Lok Sangharsh Samiti | During "the Emergency" (1975-1977)
BJP Role | Assigned to BJP by RSS | 1985
BJP Role | General Secretary | 1998
2002 Gujarat Violence | Administration considered complicit | Criticised for management
2002 Gujarat Violence | Official records: 1,000+ killed (3/4 Muslim) | Independent sources: 2,000 deaths (mostly Muslim)
2002 Gujarat Violence | Supreme Court SIT found no evidence for prosecution | 2012
Chief Minister Policies | Credited for economic growth | Criticised for failing to improve health, poverty, education indices
2014 General Election | Led BJP to parliamentary majority | First since 1984
2014 Administration Policies | Increased direct foreign investment | Reduced spending on healthcare, education, social-welfare programmes
2014 Administration Policies | Launched sanitation campaign | Weakened/abolished environmental and labour laws
Controversial Policies | Demonetisation of banknotes | 2016
Controversial Policies | Introduction of Goods and Services Tax (GST) | 2017
2019 Balakot Airstrike | Against alleged terrorist training camp in Pakistan | Action had nationalist appeal
2019 General Election | Modi's party won
Second Term Policies (2019-) | Revoked special status of Jammu and Kashmir | Introduced Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA)
Second Term Policies (2019-) | Repealed three controversial farm laws
COVID-19 Pandemic Response | Oversaw India's response | WHO estimated 4.7 million Indian deaths
2024 General Election | BJP lost majority in lower house | Formed government leading National Democratic Alliance coalition
2025 India–Pakistan Conflict | Presided over conflict | Resulted in a ceasefire
Tenure Impact | India experienced democratic backsliding | Weakening of democratic institutions, individual rights, freedom of expression
Approval Ratings | Consistently high as PM
Political Description | Engineering a political realignment towards right-wing politics
Controversial Figure | Domestically and internationally | Over Hindu nationalist beliefs and handling of Gujarat riots
Family | Father: Damodardas Mulchand Modi (c. 1915–1989) | Mother: Hiraben Modi (1923–2022)
Marriage | Betrothed to Jashodaben Chimanlal Modi | Never consummated, abandoned wife
Political Mentor | Lakshmanrao Inamdar (RSS)
First Political Activity (Adult) | Joined Jana Sangh Satyagraha in Delhi | 1971
Emergency Period (1975-1977) | Forced to go underground | Involved in printing pamphlets, organising demonstrations, creating safe houses
Book Authored (Emergency) | Sangharsh Ma Gujarat (In the Struggles of Gujarat) | Gujarati-language
BJP Gujarat Unit | Organising Secretary | 1987
National Election Committee | Member | 1990
Key Campaigns Organised | Advani's Ram Rath Yatra (1990) | Murli Manohar Joshi's Ekta Yatra (1991–1992)
BJP National Secretary | Appointed | November 1995
BJP General Secretary (Organisation) | Promoted | May 1998
Chief Minister Appointment | Replaced Keshubhai Patel | 3 October 2001
Chief Minister Sworn In | 7 October 2001
Legislative Assembly Entry | Won by-election in Rajkot II constituency | 24 February 2002
2002 Gujarat Violence Statement | "What is happening is a chain of action and reaction"
US Visa Denial | 2005 | Due to poor human-rights record (Gujarat CM)
International Relations | UK and EU lifted bans in 2012 and 2013 | US lifted ban and invited him in 2014
Longest-serving CM of Gujarat | 2,063 consecutive days | By July 2007
Chief Minister Policies | Favoured privatisation and small government | Reduced corruption
Development Projects | Established financial and technology parks | Encouraged groundwater-conservation projects
Development Projects | Offered land to Tata Motors for Nano car plant | 2008
Development Projects | Expanded Jyotigram Yojana (electricity distribution)
Economic Development (CM Tenure) | GDP growth rate averaged 10% | Strong economic growth before and during tenure
Human Development (CM Tenure) | Poor record on human development, poverty relief, nutrition, education
2014 Election Campaign Focus | Corruption scandals of previous government | Image as creator of high GDP growth in Gujarat
2014 Election Campaign Cost | Around ₹50 billion (US$590 million)
2014 Election Result | BJP won 31% of vote | 282 seats in Lok Sabha (majority)
2014 Lok Sabha Constituencies | Varanasi (won) | Vadodara (won, then vacated)
First PM born after Independence | 1947
2019 Election Campaign Slogan | Main Bhi Chowkidar ("I too am a watchman")
2019 Election Campaign Focus | Defence and national security | Development and good foreign relations
2019 Lok Sabha Constituency | Varanasi (won)
2019 Election Result | NDA won 353 seats | BJP alone won 303 seats
2024 Election Campaign Slogan | "Modi Ki Guarantee" ("Modi's assurance")
2024 Lok Sabha Constituency | Varanasi (won) | Margin of 152,513 votes
2024 Election Result | NDA secured 292 seats | BJP solely won 240 seats
PM Governance (First Year) | Significant centralisation of power | Abolished Planning Commission, replaced with NITI Aayog
PM Governance (First Year) | Launched investigations into civil society organisations and foreign NGOs
Laws Repealed | 1,200 obsolete laws in first three years as PM
Initiatives | Digital India programme
Social Policies | Law to reserve 10% of educational admission and government jobs for economically disadvantaged | 2019
Social Policies | Ujjwala scheme (free LPG connections to rural households) | 2016
Proposed Reforms | One Nation, One Election (synchronise elections) | Bill approved by Cabinet in September 2024
Criticism | Lack of reaction to Manipur violence (since May 2023)
Hindutva | Increased activities of Hindu nationalist organisations | Government support
Hindutva | Stronger links between BJP and RSS | RSS-affiliated individuals appointed to government positions
Hindutva | Changes in government-approved history textbooks
Citizenship Law | Passed in 2019 | Provides route to citizenship for non-Muslim persecuted religious minorities
Citizenship Law | First time religion overtly used as criterion for citizenship | Attracted global criticism, widespread protests
2020 Delhi Riots | Spurred by CAA protests | Muslims targeted by Hindu mobs | 53 killed (2/3 Muslim)
Ayodhya Visit | First PM to visit temples at Ram Janmabhoomi and Hanuman Garhi | 5 August 2020
Post-2019 Actions (RSS calls) | Triple Talaq made illegal | Revoked Article 370 (J&K autonomy) | Abrogated J&K statehood
2024 Election Campaign Rhetoric | Referred to Muslims as infiltrators with many children
Economic Policies | Focused on privatisation and liberalisation | Neoliberal framework
Economic Policies | Liberalised foreign direct investment policies
Economic Policies | Reduced funds for poverty-reduction and social welfare programmes
Economic Policies | Lowered corporate taxes, abolished wealth tax, increased sales taxes
Economic Policies | Deregulated diesel prices | October 2014
Economic Policies | Reduced spending on education as share of budget
Initiatives | Make in India (encourage foreign manufacturing) | September 2014
Initiatives | Land-reform bill (acquire private agricultural land) | Allowed to lapse
Tax Reform | Goods and Services Tax (GST) | Effective 1 July 2017
Black Money Initiative | Set up team to investigate black money | First cabinet decision
Demonetisation | ₹500 and ₹1000 banknotes | 9 November 2016
Demonetisation Impact | Severe cash shortages, decline in stock indices, widespread protests | Estimated 1.5 million jobs lost, 1% of GDP wiped out
Economic Performance | Decline in GDP growth and increasing joblessness compared to previous administration
Economic Performance | Average GDP growth rate of 5.5% (first 8 years) | Unemployment highest in 45 years (2017)
Health Spending | Reduced central government healthcare spending in first year as PM
Health Policy | New Health Policy (NHP) | January 2015 | Emphasised private healthcare
Sanitation Campaign | Swachh Bharat Mission ("Clean India") | 2 October 2014
Swachh Bharat Mission Goals | Elimination of open defecation and manual scavenging within five years
Swachh Bharat Mission Impact | Sanitation cover increased from 38.7% (2014) to 84.1% (2018) | WHO: 180,000 diarrhoeal deaths averted
COVID-19 Response | Invoked Epidemic Diseases Act, Disaster Management Act | March 2020
COVID-19 Response | Announced 14-hour curfew, three-week "total lockdown"
COVID-19 Vaccination | Administered 1.7 billion doses by January 2022 | 720 million fully vaccinated
Foreign Policy | Focused on improving economic ties, security, regional relations | Continued "multi-alignment"
Foreign Policy | Improved relations with US | Positive relationship with Obama and Trump administrations
Foreign Policy | Visited Nepal (promised $1 billion aid) | Visited US multiple times
Foreign Policy | Ratified land-exchange deal with Bangladesh | 2015
Foreign Policy | Renamed "Look East Policy" to "Act East Policy"
Foreign Policy | China–India relations deteriorated after 2020 skirmishes
Foreign Policy | Pledged $900 million aid to Afghanistan | Visited twice, received highest civilian honour (2016)
Foreign Policy | Strong personal relationship with Russia's Vladimir Putin (late 2022)
2023 G20 New Delhi Summit | India hosted | African Union joined G20 as permanent member
Defence Spending | Nominal military spending steadily increased | Military budget declined as fraction of GDP
Defence Initiatives | Military modernisation initiative | "Aatmanirbhar Bharat" (indigenous defence manufacturing)
Pakistan Policy | "Tough on Pakistan" | Called Pakistan an exporter of terrorism
2016 Surgical Strike | Indian Army conducted strike on terror launch pads in Azad Kashmir | Scope and casualties exaggerated by India
2019 Airstrikes | Against supposed terrorist camp in Pakistan | Open source imagery suggested no significant targets hit
2020 China–India Skirmishes | Aggressive skirmishes along Sino-Indian border
Russia Relations | Signed agreement to extend military technical cooperation | December 2021
Russia Relations | Bought S-400 missile system | Refused to condemn 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine
Operation Ganga | Initiative to return Indians stranded in Ukraine | Evacuated 19,000+ nationals
2025 India–Pakistan Conflict | Accused Pakistan of supporting cross-border terrorism | Suspended Indus Waters Treaty and bilateral trade
Environment Ministry | Renamed "Ministry of Environment, Forests, and Climate Change" | Budget reduced by more than half
Environmental Policies | Removed or diluted environmental protection laws | Lifted moratorium on new industrial activity in polluted areas
Climate Change Stance | Initially downplayed climate change (2014) | Later called for climate action, clean energy
Climate Initiatives | Proposed International Solar Alliance | 2015
Climate Targets | India to target carbon neutrality by 2070 | Expand renewable energy capacity (COP26)
Climate Achievements | Only major economy on track to meet Paris Agreement goals | Achieved 10% ethanol blending ahead of schedule
Democratic Backsliding | Weakening of democratic institutions, individual rights, freedom of expression
Democratic Backsliding | Used state power to intimidate and stifle critics in media and academia
Democratic Backsliding | Erosion of civil rights and press freedom in second term
Personal Image | Vegetarian and teetotaller
Public Interaction | First Indian politician to interact with citizens on live chat (Google Hangouts) | 31 August 2012
Appearance | Regularly wears ironed, half-sleeved kurta | Suit with name embroidered in pinstripes
Personality Description | Energetic, eccentric, arrogant, charismatic
Reputation | "One of contemporary India's most controversial and divisive politicians"
Campaign Strategy | Focused on Modi as an individual | Relied on reputation for economic growth and development
Highest Approval Rating | 88% (Pew Research poll) | End of third year in office
Most Popular Figure | In Indian politics (Pew Research Center survey) | Early 2017
Global Leader Approval | Highest net approval rating (Morning Consult) | 22 December 2020
Biographical Films | Modi Kaka Ka Gaon (2017) | PM Narendra Modi (2019)
Web Series | Modi: Journey of a Common Man (2019)
Documentaries | 7 RCR (2014) | India: The Modi Question (2023, banned by Indian government)
Other Portrayals | Rajit Kapur (Uri: The Surgical Strike) | Vikram Gokhale (Avrodh: The Siege Within)
TV Appearances | Man vs. Wild with Bear Grylls | July 2019 | Jim Corbett National Park
Radio Programme | Mann Ki Baat (monthly) | All India Radio
Student Initiative | Pariksha Pe Charcha (discussions for students)
Time Magazine Cover | March 2012, June 2014, June 2015
Awards | Indian of the Year (CNN-News18) | 2014
Awards | Time magazine's 100 Most Influential People in the World | 2014, 2015, 2017, 2020, 2021
Awards | Forbes Magazine's World's Most Powerful People | 15th (2014), 9th (2015, 2016, 2018)
Awards | Bloomberg Markets Magazine's Most Influential Person in the World | 13th (2015)
Awards | Fortune Magazine's World's Greatest Leaders | 5th (2015)
Awards | Gallup International Association (GIA) third-top leader of the world | 2017
Recognition | Wax statue unveiled at Madame Tussauds | London | 2016
Social Media Influence | Second-most-followed politician on Twitter and Facebook (2015) | Most-followed world leader on Instagram and Facebook (2018)
Awards | United Nations' Champions of the Earth | October 2018 | For policy leadership in International Solar Alliance
Awards | Seoul Peace Prize | 2018
Recognition | Picture displayed on ADNOC building facade | Abu Dhabi, UAE
Recognition | Howdy Modi community event | Houston, Texas | 22 September 2019
Awards | Key to the City of Houston | Presented by Mayor Sylvester Turner
Awards | Global Goalkeeper Award (Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation) | 24 September 2019 | For Swachh Bharat Mission
Awards | Ig Nobel Prize in Medical Education (parody) | 2020 | For COVID-19 pandemic response
Awards | Legion of Merit (US president Donald Trump) | 21 December 2020 | For improving India–United States relations
Recognition | Motera Stadium renamed Narendra Modi Stadium | 24 February 2021
Awards | Order of St. Andrew (Russia's highest civilian award) | July 2024 | For development of bilateral ties
Awards | Order of the Dragon King (Bhutan's highest decoration) | March 2024 | First non-Bhutanese head of government
Awards | Grand Commander of the Order of the Niger (Nigeria) | November 2024 | Second foreign dignitary after Queen Elizabeth II
Recognition | Symbolic "Key to the City" of Abuja | Presented by Minister Nyesom Wike | November 2024
Awards | Order of Mubarak the Great (Kuwait's highest national honour) | December 2024
Awards | Grand Commander of the Order of the Star and Key (Mauritius' highest honour) | March 2025
Awards | Sri Lanka Mitra Vibhushana (Sri Lanka's highest award to foreign leader) | April 2025
Writing Career | Authored Gujarati book Jyotipunj (profiles of RSS leaders) | 2008
Writing Career | Authored Exam Warriors (guide for children)
Writing Career | Penned lyrics of two Gujarati garba songs: Garbo, Maadi
Music Recognition | Featured speech in "Abundance in Millets" song | Nominated for 2024 Grammy Awards

Spiderman.notes:
Spider-Man | Superhero | Marvel Comics | Created by Stan Lee & Steve Ditko | First appearance: Amazing Fantasy #15 (August 1962) | Secret identity: Peter Benjamin Parker | Raised by Aunt May & Uncle Ben in Queens, NYC
Powers | Superhuman strength, speed, agility, reflexes, durability | Clinging to surfaces | Precognitive "spider-sense" | Origin: Bitten by radioactive spider | Intellect: Genius level in science & engineering
Equipment | Spider-web patterned spandex costume | Wrist-mounted "web-shooter" devices | Artificial spider-webs | Spider-tracers
Motto | "With great power comes great responsibility" | Learned after Uncle Ben's murder
Supporting characters | Aunt May | Uncle Ben | J. Jonah Jameson (Daily Bugle publisher) | Harry Osborn (best friend) | Flash Thompson (high school bully, later Agent Venom) | Robbie Robertson (Daily Bugle editor) | Romantic interests: Gwen Stacy, Mary Jane Watson, Black Cat
Enemies | Green Goblin (Norman Osborn, arch-enemy) | Doctor Octopus (Doc Ock, mad scientist) | Venom (Eddie Brock, deadliest foe) | Sandman | Chameleon | Lizard | Vulture | Kraven the Hunter | Electro | Mysterio | Kingpin (Wilson Fisk)
Key developments | Main series: The Amazing Spider-Man | Age progression: High school to late 20s | Team memberships: Avengers, Fantastic Four | Doctor Octopus became Superior Spider-Man (2012-2014) | Gwen Stacy killed by Green Goblin (1973) | Married Mary Jane Watson (1987), later erased by Mephisto in "One More Day" (2007) | Wore black symbiote costume (mid-1980s), led to Venom | "Clone Saga" (1990s) with Ben Reilly | Publicly revealed identity in Civil War (2006) | Miles Morales became Ultimate Spider-Man | "Spider-Verse" storyline (2014) introduced variants
Live-action film actors | Tobey Maguire (Sam Raimi trilogy) | Andrew Garfield (Marc Webb duology) | Tom Holland (MCU) | All three appeared in Spider-Man: No Way Home (2021)
Animated film voice actors | Jake Johnson (Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse, Across the Spider-Verse) | Chris Pine (Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse)
Cultural impact | One of the most popular & commercially successful superheroes | Cultural icon | Ranked #1 superhero by Bravo (2005) | Ranked 5th greatest comic book character by Empire | Ranked 3rd greatest comic book character by Wizard | Ranked 3rd on IGN's Top 100 Comic Book Heroes (2011) | Ranked greatest Marvel character by IGN (2014) & Comic Book Resources (2015) | Most profitable superhero (2014) with $1.3 billion in licensed product sales
Alternate versions | Spider-Man 2099 (Miguel O'Hara) | Ultimate Spider-Man (Peter Parker, Miles Morales) | Spider-Ham (Peter Porker) | Spider-Girl (Mayday Parker) | Spiderling (Annie Parker) | Spider-Man Noir | Pavitr Prabhakar (Spider-Man: India) | Spider-Gwen | Spider-UK (Billy Braddock)
Broadway musical | Spider-Man: Turn Off the Dark | Premiered: November 14, 2010 | Most expensive Broadway musical ($70 million)
